perturbation.original_text: Recipient desires access to information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to (describe CEII and Confidential Information requested) __________________ NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 2 of 7 Date : ________________ ______ __________________ ________________________________ ______________ __ for the intend us e (describe): ____________________________ ____________ ________ ________________________________________________________________________
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient desires access to information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to (describe CEII and Confidential Information requested) __________________ NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 2 of 7 Date : ________________ ______ __________________ ________________________________ ______________ __ for the intended application (describe): ____________________________ ____________ ________ ________________________________________________________________________
perturbation.explanation: The terminology is changed from 'intended use' to 'intended application'. While seemingly synonymous, their usage causes in-text contradiction on Section V where it states: The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose. Intended use and Intended application, might not be the same thing
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. Use of Confidential Information The Recipient will not knowingly use Confidential Inf ormation for an illeg al or non - legitimate purpose . NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 3 of 7 Date : ________________ The Recipient of CEII may use CEII as foundation for advice provided to others, but may not disclose CEII to another individual unless that individual is an approved Recipient of the same CEII. The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'intended use' to 'intended application' introduces a strong contradiction. The original text specified 'use' of confidential information. Section V explicitly states that the recipient will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. By changing 'use' to 'application' in Section II, it creates a potential ambiguity. 'Use' and 'application' may not be interpreted the same way, creating a contradiction regarding whether the recipient is in compliance with Section V if the 'application' differs from the originally 'intended use', potentially leading to a situation where the 'application' is not considered 'use' as originally defined. This ambiguity directly contradicts the requirement that Confidential Information be used solely for the purpose stated in Section II, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Information or any portion of it. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Data or any portion of it. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in the first sentence of Section III introduces ambiguity. The agreement consistently uses "Confidential Information," and now this change implies that 'Confidential Data' might be a subset of 'Confidential Information,' or an entirely different category. This contradicts section IV
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Standard of Care Confidential Information shall be maintained by Recipient in a secure place. Access to those materials shall be limited to other Recipients of the identical material. Recipients may make copies of Confidential Information, but such copies become Confidential Information and subject to these same procedures . Recipient s may make notes of Confidential Information which shall be treated as Confidential Information notes if they contain Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" introduces ambiguity. The original text and contradicted text consistently refer to "Confidential Information". By introducing "Confidential Data", it is unclear whether it is a subset, superset, or completely distinct from "Confidential Information". Section IV defines procedures specifically for "Confidential Information", so if "Confidential Data" is different, the procedures in Section IV might not apply to it, creating a strong contradiction as to what information the recipient is responsible for protecting and how.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall be maintained by Recipient in a secure place. Access to those materials shall be limited to other Recipients of the identical material. Recipients may make copies of Confidential Information, but such copies become Confidential Information and subject to these same procedures . Recipient s may make notes of Confidential Information which shall be treated as Confidential Information notes if they contain Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall be maintained by Recipient in a secure location. Access to those materials shall be limited to other Recipients of the identical material. Recipients may make copies of Confidential Information, but such copies become Confidential Information and subject to these same procedures . Recipient s may make notes of Confidential Information which shall be treated as Confidential Information notes if they contain Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "secure place" to "secure location" in Section IV creates ambiguity. While similar, "location" could imply a broader geographical area than "place," potentially contradicting the specific requirements for physical security implied elsewhere in the NDA, specially regarding Section III, if the location is broader than the place, it can be viewed or taken by unauthorized person.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. Non-Disclosure Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Information or any portion of it. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement . Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B. As described above, you may exercise your right of access to Confidential Information only through individuals who: (i) have requested access to Confid ential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non Disclosure Agreement by providing a signed copy of App endix A or Appendix B to GTC . You agree to keep an updated list of such individuals and to ensure that only those individuals have access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from "secure place" to "secure location" introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of security, potentially contradicting Section III, which mandates measures to prevent unauthorized viewing or access. A "location" could encompass a wider area than a "place," making it harder to ensure the Confidential Information is secure from unauthorized access as strictly required by Section III. The explanation provided states this specifically.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not knowingly use Confidential Inf ormation for an illeg al or non - legitimate purpose . NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 3 of 7 Date : ________________ The Recipient of CEII may use CEII as foundation for advice provided to others, but may not disclose CEII to another individual unless that individual is an approved Recipient of the same CEII. The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will not knowingly use Confidential Information for an illeg al or inappropriate purpose . NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 3 of 7 Date : ________________ The Recipient of CEII may use CEII as foundation for advice provided to others, but may not disclose CEII to another individual unless that individual is an approved Recipient of the same CEII. The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "non-legitimate" to "inappropriate" in Section V introduces a subjective standard. 'Illegitimate' has a legal context, whereas 'inappropriate' is more open to interpretation, potentially contradicting the explicit restrictions on use defined later in the same section. This contradiction affects the enforceability of the usage restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. Use of Confidential Information The Recipient will not knowingly use Confidential Information for an illeg al or inappropriate purpose . NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 3 of 7 Date : ________________ The Recipient of CEII may use CEII as foundation for advice provided to others, but may not disclose CEII to another individual unless that individual is an approved Recipient of the same CEII. The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'non-legitimate' to 'inappropriate' introduces a subjective standard, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. While 'illegitimate' has a basis in law, 'inappropriate' is open to interpretation, weakening the explicit restrictions on Confidential Information use defined later in Section V and contradicting the clause 'You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.' in the contradicted text.
